title: Item B — Extend infrastructure-catalog.schema.json with vpcPeerings[] under catalog
description: Additive backwards-compatible extension to luh-tech/schema-registry/schemas/infrastructure-catalog.schema.json adding vpcPeerings[] as a new optional property at the catalog level. Schema version bumps 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0 per minor-bump-for-additive convention exercised in PR #102 (terraform-state enum extension). Resolves FU-SR-CATALOG-VPC-PEERING-FIELD-1 filed in SR register 2026-06-02 via D-EB-FU-FILE (PR #103). Substrate driver: live VPC peering 768a1eb6 (staging <-> production VPCs) is flagged in operator memory for elimination; current schema has no field to mark scheduled for elimination, track environment coupling, or detect drift when removed. Shape per chat-side sandbox decisions 2026-06-03: id pattern matches existing convention (vpc-peering-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*), envA/envB carry env-id pattern for FK validation, region required (DO peerings are region-scoped), doId separate field captures upstream DO resource id (mirrors server/database pattern). Optional createdAt + notes fields mirror existing environment.notes pattern. eliminationTargetDate skipped — trackedByFollowUp carries that schedule via the linked FU. Unblocks EB Cat 3b drift-vpcpeering tool (deferred from Cat 3 partial d-2026-06-03-eb-agent-cat3-coverage-drift-tools-partial OUT-1).

outputs.description: Schema extension on schemas/infrastructure-catalog.schema.json. Five edits applied atomically: (1) version field bumped 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0; (2) new property vpcPeerings added to catalog.properties as type: array with item schema below; (3) catalog.additionalProperties: false continues to enforce (no other property added); (4) vpcPeerings NOT added to catalog.required — optional field; (5) audit notes updated. Item schema: object, additionalProperties: false, required [id, envA, envB, region, status], properties: id (pattern ^vpc-peering-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$), doId (string, upstream DO resource id), envA (pattern ^env-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$, FK to environments[].id), envB (pattern ^env-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$, FK to environments[].id), region (string, cloud region e.g. sfo3), status (enum: active, pending-elimination, eliminated), trackedByFollowUp (pattern ^FU-[A-Z][A-Z0-9-]+-[0-9]+$), createdAt (format: date), notes (string). Resolves FU-SR-CATALOG-VPC-PEERING-FIELD-1 (filed-and-closed in same PR per PR #102 precedent).
acceptanceCriteria: vpcPeerings.items requires [id, envA, envB, region, status]
vpcPeerings.items.properties includes optional: doId, trackedByFollowUp, createdAt, notes
Schema validates as draft-07 (ajv-cli@5.0.0 with --strict=false; meta-schema-check.yml passes)
Existing schema fields and structure preserved exactly outside the additions above
Empty existing instances (if any) continue to validate against bumped schema (backwards-compatible verified)

Full ajv validation against follow-up-register.schema.json passes with canonical --strict=false -c ajv-formats invocation

transitions.trigger: Spec authored after substrate-read on SR main f4ab281 (post-Cat 3 instance landing PR #105). Three structural decisions confirmed: vpcPeerings lives at catalog level (sibling to environments) not per-environment — VPC peering is a relationship between two envs, not a property of either; canonical id pattern vpc-peering-[a-z][a-z0-9-]* matches existing schema convention; envA/envB carry env-id pattern for ajv-level FK validation. Region required; doId captures upstream DO id; createdAt + notes optional mirroring environment.notes shape; eliminationTargetDate skipped (FU carries schedule). Per Erik approval 2026-06-03 'this all looks correct'.

notes: Second cross-chat-driven SR work after D-EB-FU-FILE (PR #103) and D-SR-CAT3-INSTANCE (PR #105). Resolves FU-SR-CATALOG-VPC-PEERING-FIELD-1 (filed yesterday in SR register from EB cross-chat handoff canvas F0B7U37MSDC + work brief F0B7UB6BGR4). Schema version bump pattern: minor for additive backwards-compatible per PR #102 precedent (1.0.0 -> 1.1.0 for terraform-state enum, now 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0 for vpcPeerings). After this merges, Item A livenessProbe is the only remaining canvas F0B7UB6BGR4 deliverable; that resource-access.schema.json edit follows separately. After both Item A + B merge, EB Cat 3b unblocks (final 2 eb-agent tools, surface 29 -> 31). Filed-and-closed FU pattern from PR #102 is the canonical shape — the schema extension and its tracking FU resolve in the same atomic PR. Three downstream consumers: EB d-2026-06-XX-eb-catalog-vpc-peerings-instance (EB populates 768a1eb6 as pending-elimination linking staging+prod env ids), EB Cat 3b drift-vpcpeering tool, EB future agent tooling that checks for unexpected peerings.
